value_raw: A genuine chilling effect and visible anxiety within the foreign-resident community, directly and currently
notes: Social-media-monitoring enforcement means visible content-creation/brand-partnership self-documentation is now a direct legal-risk source — an active, ongoing shift in how the community presents itself, not speculative

value_raw: 28-33°C daytime year-round; wet season Oct-Mar, drier Apr-Sep
notes: Not independently checked against a primary meteorological source
Noticeably cheaper than Canggu/Seminyak for the same lifestyle

Canggu/Seminyak-weighted figure, incl. private villa/apartment, meals out, scooter, social lifestyle. Wider range across lifestyle tiers: $1,000-4,500/month. Runs 40-70% lower than major Western cities

fact_label: Conditions triggering medevac (beyond general "serious cases") — Singapore
value_raw: Major cardiac surgery, severe trauma, complex neurological procedures, specialized cancer treatment; decompression sickness from diving often unavailable locally
notes: Decompression-sickness gap relevant given Uluwatu's surf/dive culture. Distance/cost of the medevac itself already extracted separately (see the two rows above this section)

value_raw: Yes (backup generators standard)
notes: Implies power reliability is imperfect enough that coworking spaces build around it as a matter of course — a different infrastructure baseline than this project's European candidates

value_raw: Yes
notes: Rising in 2026 specifically, linked to tourism growth

value_raw: 40-70 minutes drive time in evening traffic
notes: BIMC Kuta usually the closest private after-hours option (24hr A&E, ICU, radiology); Ubud has its own option, Prima Medika Hospital

value_raw: The deepest digital-nomad saturation in this project — Canggu a 'digital nomad capital' reference point for close to a decade
notes: 20+ dedicated coworking spaces in Canggu alone (Dojo Bali, Outpost, Tropical Nomad named); Hubud in Ubud, BWork in Berawa. The deepest, most mature nomad-community infrastructure of any candidate in this project

value_raw: Closed to all hiking until April 24, 2026 for Karya Ida Bhatara Turun Kabeh ceremonies at Besakih Temple; reopened April 25, 2026

fact_label: International health insurance — enrollment age ceiling
notes: Provider-dependent; one provider (IMG Global) cited to 80

fact_label: International health insurance — premium range by age
notes: Covers pre-existing conditions and medical evacuation, unlike the local tier
Business-grade 150-300 Mbps available (IndiHome/CBN) at $40-80/month — the best internet infrastructure of any Bali neighborhood
Vs. Bangkok's 60+ — a quantified capability-tier gap relative to the region's medical-tourism hub

value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Not researched this pass.
A lease-term price, not a freehold purchase price

fact_label: Local private health insurance — new-enrollment age ceiling
notes: Notably harsher than the 65-75 ceiling commonly cited elsewhere in this project. Compounds directly with Retirement KITAS's own 55+/60+ age floor

fact_label: Local private health insurance — premium range by age
notes: Annual caps ~$30,000-60,000; pre-existing conditions typically excluded

fact_label: Local private hospital system capability — routine/intermediate care
value_raw: BIMC/Siloam/Kasih Ibu handle minor surgery, fracture care, dengue monitoring, maternity/pediatric care, general cardiology/oncology/orthopedics/neurology/ENT consultation; BIMC Kuta runs 24hr A&E + 3-bed ICU w/ isolation bed
notes: Real competent capability for the non-serious-case tier

value_raw: 2 hours by air; AUD 30,000-50,000 for an air ambulance
notes: The described standard of care for genuinely serious cases — a materially different baseline than any EU candidate in this project, comparable in kind (not degree) to Atitlán's 2.5-3hr emergency-care distance

fact_label: No first-hand multi-year foreign-resident account (esp. re: 2026 enforcement climate)
value_raw: [GAP]
notes: Genuinely unclear whether a compliant, documented visa holder (Second Home/Retirement KITAS) faces real practical friction from the crackdown, or whether it's targeted specifically at violators — the same "statutory breadth vs. operational practice" distinction Portugal's UNEF research modeled
Not particularly high, but composite obscures a real distinction: violent crime against foreigners is genuinely low, lower than many major Western cities

value_raw: Yes
notes: The single most frequently reported crime — commonly phones held near roads while walking/scootering
Rents risen ~10-25% annually since 2021, driven by international-relocation/digital-nomad demand — a fast-appreciating rental market, not a stable one

value_raw: Bali's non-freehold property structure likely shapes a compound/adjacent-parcel model very differently than any freehold-based candidate — not evaluated this pass
notes: A leasehold or Hak Pakai arrangement for several adjacent villa parcels is plausible in principle but genuinely untested

value_raw: [GAP]
source_ref: [GAP — not yet checked this pass; highest-priority open question]
notes: Bali sits within the Pacific Ring of Fire; a real, named gap distinct from Mount Agung specifically

value_raw: Yes
notes: Named repeatedly as a genuine, significant risk category tied to Bali's scooter-centric transport culture — a real, distinct hazard, not folded into "crime"

value_raw: Alert Level 1 — Normal (lowest of 4-level scale); last eruption June 13, 2019; unrest (vapor/smoke) reported through late 2025
notes: Per CVGHM/PVMBG (Indonesia's own volcanology agency), status reported via secondary sources rather than the bulletin fetched directly. A real, active, currently-quiet volcano, not dormant/extinct — the same first-class treatment this project gives Guatemala's Fuego

value_raw: Yes (active, escalating)
notes: 100-person task force, 10 patrolled areas incl. Canggu/Seminyak; 62 detentions in first 3 weeks; 331 deportations from Ngurah Rai alone in 2025. "Unauthorized work" deliberately widened to include unpaid promotional/sponsored content — money changing hands is no longer the operative test. Social-media monitoring is explicit enforcement policy. The sharpest, most rapidly escalating enforcement pattern found anywhere in this project — comparable in kind to Thailand's nominee-business crackdown

value_raw: Legal drinking age 21 nationally, applied uniformly regardless of home-country age; majority-Muslim nation, more restricted/taxed in many regions
notes: Bali specifically (majority-Hindu, huge tourism economy) is meaningfully more relaxed — see Bali-scoped row. Methanol-poisoning risk from unregulated local spirits is a real, physical safety wrinkle, not just a legal-culture note

value_raw: 60 days initial, extendable twice more (60 days each), total max 180 days (~6 months)
notes: Increasingly relabeled "C1" under newer categorization, same visa type. Each extension requires in-person Kantor Imigrasi visit for biometric scanning, initiated 14+ days before expiry with a sponsor/agency

value_raw: Non-rabies-free-origin pets (incl. US) must enter via a mainland port (most commonly Jakarta Soekarno-Hatta), undergo quarantine there, then transport onward to Bali
notes: A real, meaningful extra step not present for a direct-to-Bali arrival

fact_label: BPJS Kesehatan eligibility — Retirement KITAS holder
value_raw: No
notes: "Foreign retirees are NOT eligible to enroll in BPJS Kesehatan" (expat.id)

fact_label: BPJS Kesehatan eligibility — Second Home Visa (E33) holder
value_raw: No
notes: Non-working visa index, same structural exclusion as retirees per multiple sources

fact_label: BPJS Kesehatan eligibility — source conflict (6-month-residency-only claim)
value_raw: Not yet checked
notes: One source (stampednomad.com) describes 6-months-residency as sufficient without the index-312 employer-sponsorship qualifier other sources state; unreconciled against a primary BPJS source this pass
Class One/Two/Three tiers; class choice is difficult to change later

fact_label: BPJS Kesehatan (public health system) eligibility — Indonesian-employer-sponsored working KITAS (index 312), 6+ months
value_raw: Yes
notes: The one clearly-eligible route found. Not a route this project's passive/remote income archetypes use (visa-legal.md)

value_raw: Group I narcotic (Law 35/2009); personal use up to 4yr; possession up to 12yr + fines to 8B rupiah (~$560k); trafficking 1kg+/5+ plants: death penalty, life, or 5-20yr
notes: 2026 Criminal Code (KUHP, effective Jan 2026) made no change to narcotics penalties. At least 143 people sentenced to death for drug offenses in 2025 alone, 400+ on death row. No medical cannabis exception exists. A genuine hard-stop risk category, not a gray zone — the strictest regime checked in this project

fact_label: E33G accepts passive income as qualifying proof
value_raw: No
notes: Foreign-income-only, cannot work for Indonesian companies or accept Indonesian-client payment. Directly implicated by the 2026 enforcement crackdown when someone does E33G-shaped work on a tourist visa without the permit

value_raw: 1 year, multiple entries, extendable annually up to 5 years total (4 extensions), then convertible to KITAP

fact_label: E33G Remote Worker Visa — health insurance requirement
value_raw: Yes — international health insurance specifically required; travel insurance not accepted
notes: Not officially listed on the immigration portal itself but standard across KITAS agent checklists

fact_label: E33G Remote Worker Visa — income threshold
notes: From an employment contract/client relationship with a company outside Indonesia. Bank statement showing $2,000+ ending balance over the last 3 months also required

fact_label: Employer-sponsored work KITAS / Student KITAS / Marriage KITAS
value_raw: [GAP]
source_ref: [GAP — named for completeness, not independently verified to a primary source this pass]
notes: Marriage KITAS: available to a foreign spouse of an Indonesian citizen

value_raw: No
notes: A fundamentally different legal starting point than every other candidate in this project (Guatemala/Colombia/Mexico/Argentina/Belize/Morocco/Portugal/Spain all permit some direct foreign freehold ownership). This project's lowest Land/property access score to date

value_raw: Only available to KITAS/KITAP holders — 30yr initial, renewable 20yr, extendable final 30yr (80yr total potential tenure)
notes: A real chicken-and-egg sequencing point — residency has to come first via a visa route before this property mechanism becomes available. Usually limited to one residence, above a minimum value threshold (not independently pulled)

value_raw: [GAP]
source_ref: [GAP — not independently verified to a primary source this pass]
notes: Requires establishing/holding shares in a PT PMA with a minimum capital threshold; commonly cited figures not cross-checked

value_raw: 25-30 years plus extension options, no residency requirement
notes: The most accessible route, most international villa buyers actually use this. Entry prices commonly cited from ~$179,000

fact_label: Legal property routes (leasehold/Hak Pakai/PT PMA) — paper-vs-practice divergence state
value_raw: Not yet checked
notes: Distinct from the already-Confirmed-diverges nominee pattern above — no lived first-hand account of a foreigner completing one of the legal routes found this pass

value_raw: Yes (illegal, criminally exposed)
notes: Indonesian courts have consistently annulled nominee structures as breaching the Basic Agrarian Law, with no legal recourse if the nominee later claims the property. An estimated 10,500 Bali properties currently held this way. Bali's own Perda 4/2026 has added criminal exposure on top of civil unenforceability — "don't do this, it can be a crime," not just "won't hold up." No safe nominee arrangement exists

value_raw: IDR 1,000,000/day (~$65 USD/day); auto-deportation + 6mo-2yr re-entry ban past 60 days
notes: Strictly enforced as of 2026 — notably harsher, more automatic than Spain's or Portugal's fine-first approach

fact_label: Pet import (dog and cat) — rabies titer / quarantine requirement
value_raw: Microchip then rabies vaccine 30 days-1yr before export; FAVN titer test required from most origins (≥0.5 IU/ml); import permit required in advance; 7-14 day quarantine
notes: No divergence between species for core requirements. Unlike Spain's US-origin titer waiver, Indonesia does NOT appear to extend an approved-country waiver. Materially harder/slower than every European candidate in this project. Recommended planning: 3-4 months ahead

value_raw: Prabowo positions Indonesia as a 'calm investment bet' (stability, fiscal discipline); military/police moving into senior political roles; rupiah became Asia's worst-performing currency in 2026, Jakarta Composite Index at lowest since May 2021
notes: A genuinely live, unresolved tension between stated pro-investment posture and concrete signals pointing the other direction — not a clean stability or instability story. 2025 growth (5%) already lagged government targets, attributed to regulatory uncertainty

value_raw: A foreign-owned Indonesian LLC holds HGB title on the property's behalf — company, not individual, is legal owner
notes: Does not itself require the owner to hold Indonesian residency. No specific setup-cost/minimum-capital figure independently pulled

fact_label: Retirement KITAS — mandatory health insurance requirement
value_raw: Yes
notes: Compounds with the visa's own 55+/60+ age floor against the local private-insurer new-enrollment ceiling below

fact_label: Retirement KITAS converts to permanent residency
value_raw: Yes
notes: Valid 1 year, renewable annually up to 5 years, then eligible for KITAP (itself valid 5 years, renewable)

Age 55+ (some sources cite 60+ depending on nationality — an unresolved variance). Mandatory health insurance, a local sponsor, and a requirement to hire Indonesian domestic staff — a genuinely unusual mandatory-hire condition not found in this project's other candidates. Prohibits employment
A 5-year version, usable as an alternative for someone below the $3,000/month income bar

fact_label: Second Home Visa — health insurance requirement
value_raw: Yes — valid in Indonesia for the full visa period, each dependent needs own policy
notes: Named application requirement, not optional

fact_label: Second Home Visa accepts passive income as qualifying proof
value_raw: N/A — asset/deposit test, not an income test; explicitly non-working but permits remote work for foreign companies
notes: Permits residing, managing personal affairs, property ownership (via the mechanisms below); no local employment or Indonesian-client income

fact_label: Second Home Visa converts to permanent residency
value_raw: Yes
notes: ITAP after 3 years continuous legal residency — genuinely faster than Portugal's/Spain's 5-year EU tracks. Not yet independently traced to Indonesian statutory text

value_raw: 5 or 10 years; 5-year version renewable once for another 5 years
notes: Dependents can join via a Dependent Second Home Visa matching the main visa's duration

value_raw: $130,000 deposit in a state-owned Indonesian bank, OR $1,000,000 Indonesian property ownership
notes: The closest Indonesian analogue to Spain's NLV/Portugal's D7, but a lump-sum wealth test, not a monthly-income test — a materially different qualification shape than every other passive-income visa in this project. Deposit earns interest, remains for the visa's duration

fact_label: "Sex outside marriage" criminal provision (2026 Criminal Code)
value_raw: Up to 1yr imprisonment (sex outside marriage), up to 6mo (unmarried cohabitation); applies to foreigners on Indonesian territory
notes: Enforcement is complaint-based only — only a legal spouse/parent/child of one of the people involved may file, not a hotel employee/neighbor/third party; Deputy Justice Minister has stated no raids on this basis. Human Rights Watch flags selective-enforcement risk. Genuinely relevant to a solo-relocation profile

fact_label: Tourist time counts toward residency clock
value_raw: No
notes: VOA/B211A/C1 time is explicitly non-working, tourist-status time — no nationality-specific exception found

value_raw: 30 days, extendable once (+30 days), total max 60 continuous days
notes: ~100 countries qualify incl. US/UK/Australia/most EU. Extension must be initiated 14+ days before expiry
